name: gmickel-claude-marketplace
description: Claude Code plugins for reliable AI coding. Flow-Next: plan-first workflows, Ralph autonomous mode (overnight coding with fresh context), multi-model review gates via RepoPrompt/Codex, re-anchoring to prevent drift, receipt-based gating.

commit.message: chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.26.0
fix(flow-next): set Codex agent reasoning to high (not medium)

feat(flow-next): update Codex models to gpt-5.4

- Review/oracle: gpt-5.2 → gpt-5.4 (high reasoning)
- Agent intelligent tier: gpt-5.3-codex → gpt-5.4 (medium reasoning)
- Fast scouts: keep gpt-5.3-codex-spark (unchanged)

docs(flow-next): remove incorrect Ralph support claim from Codex section

Ralph requires plugin hooks (guard hooks, receipt gating) which Codex
doesn't support. Removed from "What works" list and expanded caveats.
chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.25.0

Merge pull request #93 from acebytes/fix/embed-files-on-unix-codex-reviews

Thanks @acebytes for the fix! Clean solution with the budget-aware files_embedded logic.

commit.message:
fix: allow disk reads when embed budget is exceeded

Address review feedback: when get_embedded_file_contents() skips files
due to budget exhaustion or truncates them, setting files_embedded=True
would tell Codex "don't read from disk" — leaving it with incomplete
context and risking false verdicts.

Now files_embedded is derived from actual embed results:
- True only when no files were budget-skipped or truncated
- False (allow disk reads) when embedding was incomplete

This gives the best of both worlds: embedded files reduce turn waste
in the common case, but Codex can still read from disk as a fallback
when the budget doesn't cover everything.

fix: always embed file contents in codex reviews on all platforms

On Unix/macOS, codex reviews (plan-review, impl-review,
completion-review) did not embed file contents in the prompt — only
Windows did. This caused Codex to spend its entire turn budget reading
source files via sed/rg commands, exhausting turns before producing a
verdict.

Observed on a complex epic (fn-9, 290-line spec + 5 task specs):
- 114 shell command executions reading source files
- 3.68M input tokens consumed
- 4/4 todo items still uncompleted when turn.completed fired
- No verdict produced (review failed)

Changes:
- Remove os.name == "nt" gate from all three review commands
  (impl-review, plan-review, completion-review)
- Always call get_embedded_file_contents() regardless of platform
- Set files_embedded = True so the prompt instructs Codex to use
  embedded content instead of exploring the filesystem
- Increase default FLOW_CODEX_EMBED_MAX_BYTES from 100KB to 500KB
  to accommodate larger codebases with many source files

The existing FLOW_CODEX_EMBED_MAX_BYTES env var still allows users
to tune the budget. The sandbox read-only mode on Unix remains
unchanged as a defense-in-depth measure.
chore(flow-next): update local setup to v0.24.0

feat(flow-next): spec-driven workflow guidance in CLAUDE.md snippet

Add "Creating a spec" section to claude-md-snippet.md template with
structured spec template and trigger phrases. Update README with
spec-driven workflow as recommended entry point.

feat(flow-next): sync browser skill with upstream agent-browser

Add version check, command chaining, annotated screenshots, diff
commands, eval --stdin/-b, session persistence with encryption,
iOS simulator, proxy, profiling, video recording, and 4 new
reference files from vercel-labs/agent-browser.

fix(flow-next): --create skips empty RP window without folder

When only an empty default window exists, setup-review --create
reused it instead of creating a workspace with the repo folder.
Builder failed with "No workspace open". Now falls through to
workspace creation path.

Bump 0.22.2 → 0.22.3

docs(flow-next): add RepoPrompt model setup instructions

Context builder + chat models must be configured in RP UI (not
CLI-controllable). Document recommended models and setup steps.
docs: add Codex install tip to root README
docs(codex): note project-scoped install blocked on openai/codex#4734
chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.22.2

fix(codex): ensure multi_agent at TOML root + dedup [agents] table

- Prepend multi_agent = true before any [table] header so it stays
  at root scope (not nested under a preceding table)
- Skip if already set anywhere (features.multi_agent also works)
- Only declare [agents] if not already present in user config
- Prevents invalid TOML from duplicate [agents] declarations
- Clean old entries before generating new ones (idempotent)
docs(codex): add clone instructions to install steps

feat(codex): multi-agent roles for Codex 0.102.0+ (#92)

* feat(codex): rewrite install script for multi-agent roles (0.102.0+)

Codex custom multi-agent roles replace the old frontmatter conversion:
- Agent .md → .toml role configs (model, developer_instructions)
- config.toml entries auto-generated with descriptions + config_file
- Model mapping: opus → gpt-5.3-codex, sonnet → gpt-5.3-codex-spark
  except: epic-scout, claude-md-scout, docs-gap-scout → gpt-5.3-codex
  (these do reasoning/judgment, not just config scanning)
- Spark agents skip model_reasoning_effort (not supported)
- max_threads=12 for parallel scout execution
- Work/plan skills patched for native role invocations
- Removed old subagent-removal hacks (workers + scouts run natively)

* fix(codex): add multi_agent flag, rename claude-md → agents-md scout

- Set multi_agent = true in config.toml (required by Codex 0.102.0+)
- Rename claude-md-scout → agents-md-scout (Codex uses AGENTS.md)
- Patch body: CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md, Claude Code → Codex
- Split model tiers: 3 smart scouts on gpt-5.3-codex, 8 fast on Spark

* fix(flow-next): patch prime scouts for codex + auto-create RP workspace

- Add patch_prime_for_codex_agents() to install-codex.sh (was missing,
  causing "Scout availability partial" — only 4/9 matched)
- Add --create to all setup-review calls so RP auto-opens workspace
  if no window matches (impl/plan/epic-review + export-context)
- Fix stale model guidance in prime workflow (haiku → sonnet)
- Bump 0.22.0 → 0.22.1

* fix(codex): escape backslashes in TOML scout configs + update docs

- install-codex.sh: escape backslashes before writing agent body to TOML
  (fixes 5/9 scouts failing due to regex patterns in developer_instructions)
- README: update Codex section to near-parity, add /prompts: prefix mapping,
  fix model reference for review backend

* docs(codex): document 3-tier model mapping + scout roles

- CLAUDE.md: model table with intelligent/smart/fast tiers + agent list
- README: model mapping table, override examples, caveats section
- install-codex.sh: header comments updated for prime patching + tiers

commit.message:
fix(flow-next): receipt-reset false positive + block self-modification

PostToolUse receipt-write detection matched codex commands containing
--receipt path + > chars from <verdict> tags, resetting review state
immediately after setting it. Now uses proper shell redirect regexes.

Also blocks Edit/Write to ralph-guard.py, flowctl.py, flowctl, and
hooks.json during Ralph runs to prevent agents from bypassing guards.

ralph-guard v0.13.0

feat(flow-next): upgrade scout agents from haiku to claude-sonnet-4-6

All 11 lightweight scout subagents now pinned to claude-sonnet-4-6
instead of haiku. Better reasoning, instruction following, training
data cutoff Jan 2026. Requires Claude Code 2.1.45+.

feat(flow-next): make github-scout opt-in (default: false)

Disabled by default via scouts.github config. Enable via /flow-next:setup
or flowctl config set scouts.github true. Reduces planning cost and
removes gh CLI requirement for users who don't need cross-repo search.
docs: link Codex install section from main README callout
chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.20.20

fix(flow-next): reorder review workflows to prevent double context build

Move context-gathering phase before Atomic Setup Block in all three
review skills (impl-review, plan-review, epic-review). Builder now
runs once with a real summary instead of a placeholder. Added guardrails
against re-running setup-review.
fix(codex): rewrite work phases patch using heredoc + sed (fixes quoting)
fix(codex): simplify RP wait instructions - no background jobs
fix(codex): clean up flow-gap-analyst subagent reference
fix(codex): patch scout refs to ~/.codex/agents/ paths for subagents
fix(codex): add background job + stdout watch pattern for RP commands
fix(codex): correct RP progress description (stdout not window)
fix(codex): add CRITICAL DO NOT RETRY warnings for RP commands
fix(codex): enforce mandatory reviews in Codex install
docs(flow-next): add 0.20.19 changelog entry

fix(flow-next): project-local ralph-guard for cross-platform hooks

- hooks.json now references scripts/ralph/hooks/ralph-guard.py
- ralph-init copies ralph-guard.py to project during setup
- existence check ensures silent exit if ralph not initialized
- works on both Claude Code and Factory Droid without plugin root vars

fix(flow-next): hooks use shell check for cross-platform support

Hook commands now use [ -n "${VAR}" ] && ... to skip execution when
the platform variable isn't set. Eliminates noisy errors from
unexpanded variables on the other platform.

fix(flow-next): hooks use duplicate entries for cross-platform support

Droid doesn't support bash fallback syntax ${VAR:-default} in hook
commands. Now uses separate entries for CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT and
DROID_PLUGIN_ROOT. Each platform expands its own; other fails silently.
docs(flow-next): add Factory Droid native support section

fix(flow-next): cross-platform variable support for Claude Code + Droid

- hooks.json: Use ${DROID_PLUGIN_ROOT:-${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}} fallback
- hooks.json: Combined Bash|Execute regex matcher
- 17 skills: Same fallback pattern for FLOWCTL paths

fix(flow-next): restore read-only scout permissions with disallowedTools

v0.20.14 removed tools: field for cross-platform compat but gave scouts
Edit/Write access. Now use disallowedTools blacklist instead:
- 18 scouts: Edit, Write, Task (read-only)
- worker: Task only (full access)
- plan-sync: Task, Write, Bash (can Edit specs)
fix(flow-next): full Droid compatibility - remove explicit tools field
fix(flow-next): Droid Bash/Execute tool compatibility

fix(flow-next): Droid agent tool compatibility

Added FetchUrl alongside WebFetch in 7 agents.
Droid uses FetchUrl, Claude Code uses WebFetch - now both work.

Fixes "partially loaded" issue on Factory Droid.
docs: clarify Droid command autocomplete status
docs: add Factory Droid compatibility notice to README
chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.20.11
chore: reorder plugins (flow-next first for Droid auto-install)

fix(flow-next): Factory Droid compatibility

Plugin version checks now handle both:
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json (Claude Code)
- .factory-plugin/plugin.json (Factory Droid)

Skills gracefully fallback when either exists.

fix(ralph): cleaner branch names, ZSH-safe truncation

- Branch format: ralph-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-rand (no PII/hostname/email/PID)
- Full verbose ID preserved in progress.txt for debugging
- Added truncate_file() helper using `: > "$file"` for ZSH compatibility

Fixes #90. Thanks @aleparreira!
chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.20.8

fix(flow-next): strengthen workflow.md directive in review SKILL.md files

- Add ⚠️ STOP directive to force reading workflow.md
- Explicitly say 'do not proceed here until workflow.md phases are complete'
- Reduces risk of agent skipping workflow.md

fix(flow-next): remove duplicate RP workflow code from SKILL.md files

- SKILL.md now references workflow.md instead of duplicating code
- Prevents agent from running setup-review/chat-send twice
- Affects epic-review, impl-review, plan-review skills

fix(codex): add timeout warning for chat-send command

- Warn that flowctl rp chat-send waits for LLM (2-5 minutes)
- Update workflow.md, SKILL.md, flowctl-reference.md

fix(codex): add timeout warnings for setup-review command

- Warn that flowctl rp setup-review takes 5-10 minutes
- Patch workflow.md, SKILL.md, flowctl-reference.md
- Prevents Codex from thinking command is stuck

fix(codex): remove subagent spawning from flow-next-work

- Inline all worker phases directly into section 3c
- Remove worker.md from skill directory
- Replace all worker/subagent references with direct implementation
- Codex lacks Task tool so can't spawn subagents

feat(codex): add agent frontmatter conversion to install script

- Convert Claude Code frontmatter to Codex format
- Remove: tools, disallowedTools, color
- Add: profile, approval_policy, sandbox_mode
- Transform model to gpt-5.2-codex-medium (configurable via env)
- Install agents to ~/.codex/agents/
- Update CLAUDE.md with agent conversion docs

fix(flow-next): prevent epic ID collisions from orphaned specs

- scan_max_epic_id now scans specs/*.md in addition to epics/*.json
- validate --all detects and reports ID collisions (fn-N used by multiple)
- validate --all warns about orphaned specs without matching epic JSON

fix(flow-next): add baseline/verify gates, fix memory docs

- Add baseline check at end of Phase 1 (run tests before implementing)
- Add verification step in Phase 5 (run tests before marking done)
- Soften Phase 2 rule (no longer suggests running tests during impl)
- Fix README: worker reads memory files directly, not via memory-scout

Closes #88
chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.20.5
fix(flow-next): hide duplicate skills from menu via user-invocable: false
chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.20.4

feat(flow-next): add epic set-title command for renaming epics

- Renames epic ID slug based on new title
- Updates all related files (epic JSON, spec, tasks)
- Updates depends_on references within epic tasks
- Updates depends_on_epics in other epics
- Handles state files and checkpoints
- Adds 7 smoke tests for full coverage

feat(flow-next): readable epic IDs with slugified titles (#87)

* feat(flow-next): add slugify and update epic ID format

- Add slugify() function using Django pattern (stdlib only)
- Update epic create to use slugified title as suffix
- Fallback to random 3-char suffix if slugify returns empty
- Update regex patterns to support longer slug suffixes in:
  - parse_id() for ID parsing
  - scan_max_epic_id() for epic number allocation
  - cmd_next() for epic listing
  - validate command for epic discovery
- Max slug length 40 chars with word-boundary truncation

Task: fn-23-zgk.1

* fix(flow-next): address review feedback for slugify

- Fix underscore handling: convert to hyphens (output only [a-z0-9-])
- Improve truncation: preserve word boundary when possible
- Update .flow/bin/flowctl.py to match plugin copy (parity):
  - Add slugify() function with unicodedata import
  - Update parse_id() regex for slug suffixes
  - Update scan_max_epic_id() regex
  - Update cmd_epic_create() to use slugify
  - Update cmd_next() regex
  - Update validate command regex

Task: fn-23-zgk.1

* feat(flow-next): update ralph-guard ID regex for slug format

- Update parse_receipt_path() regex to support variable-length slugs
- Maintains backwards compatibility with legacy fn-N and fn-N-xxx formats
- Adds support for new fn-N-longer-slug format

Task: fn-23-zgk.2

* feat(flow-next): update error messages and CLI help for slug format

- Update 31 "Expected format" error messages with descriptive examples
- Update 31 CLI help strings to show slug format examples
- Epic format: fn-N or fn-N-slug (e.g., fn-1, fn-1-add-auth)
- Task format: fn-N.M or fn-N-slug.M (e.g., fn-1.2, fn-1-add-auth.2)
- Keep backwards compat docs for short suffix format

Task: fn-23-zgk.3

* fix(flow-next): standardize error format strings

Replace bracket-style fn-N[-slug] with explicit fn-N or fn-N-slug format
for consistency with other error messages.

Task: fn-23-zgk.3

* docs(flow-next): update ID format examples to descriptive slugs

- Replace random 3-char suffix examples (fn-1-abc) with descriptive
  slugs (fn-1-add-oauth, fn-2-fix-login-bug) throughout documentation
- Update README ID Format section with new format explanation
- Update flowctl.md ID Format section with backwards compat note
- Update usage.md templates with new format and legacy support
- Update all SKILL.md files (~11 files) with new ID examples
- Update phases.md and steps.md with new format references

Task: fn-23-zgk.4

* fix(flow-next): address review feedback for doc examples

- Update flowctl.md epic create output to show fn-N-slug format
- Update file structure examples to use fn-N-slug instead of fn-N
- Add backwards compat note mentioning fn-N-xxx in usage.md files
- Update command examples to use fn-1-add-oauth instead of fn-1
- Fix flow-next SKILL.md epic create return comment
- Update sync skill input format and error messages
- Add fn-N-xxx to legacy format mentions in plan/interview/work skills

Task: fn-23-zgk.4

* fix(flow-next): final doc consistency fixes

- Update flow-next-sync input contract to include legacy formats
- Update flow-next SKILL.md "Show me task X" to use slug example
- Update flow-next SKILL.md "What tasks are there" examples

Task: fn-23-zgk.4

* fix: address completion review gaps for slug ID format

* fix: update remaining vendored flowctl error messages for slug format

* chore: set epic fn-23-zgk completion review status to ship

* chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.20.3

commit.message:
feat(flow-next): add task set-deps command

Convenience command to set multiple dependencies:
  flowctl task set-deps fn-1.3 --deps fn-1.1,fn-1.2

Matches --deps syntax from task create. Fixes common model hallucination
where agents call non-existent "task set-deps" instead of "dep add".
chore(flow): update setup_version to 0.20.1

feat(flow-next): add flow-next-deps skill for epic dependency visualization (#85)

Add new `flow-next-deps` skill for visualizing epic dependencies, blocking chains, and execution phases.

**Features:**
- Step 1: Gather epic data with dependencies via flowctl
- Step 2: Identify blocking chains (pure jq, cross-shell compatible)
- Step 3: Compute parallel execution phases
- Quick one-liner for fast status checks

**Fixes applied during review:**
- Rewrote to use `$FLOWCTL` instead of raw jq on `.flow/` files
- Fixed jq null key error in phase computation
- Rewrote Step 2 from bash associative arrays to jq (zsh compatibility)
- Updated manifest skill counts (14 → 16)

Co-authored-by: Claire Novotny <claire@novotny.org>

commit.message: docs(flow-next): add epic-review to workflow flowcharts
chore(flow): close epic fn-22-53k

feat(flow-next): add epic-completion review gate (#84)

* feat(flowctl): add completion_review_status field + selector

- Add completion_review_status/completion_reviewed_at defaults in normalize_epic()
- Add epic set-completion-review-status command (mirrors plan-review-status pattern)
- Add --require-completion-review flag to flowctl next
- Return status=completion_review when all tasks done but review not shipped

Task: fn-22-53k.1

* feat(flow-next): add epic-review command shim

- Invokes flow-next-epic-review skill
- Follows plan-review.md pattern exactly

Task: fn-22-53k.2

* feat(flowctl): add codex completion-review command

- Add build_completion_review_prompt for two-phase spec verification

commit.message: feat(flow-next): plan fn-21-rqz epic

feat(flow-next): plan epic-completion review gate (fn-22-53k)

Add review gate that runs when all epic tasks complete, before epic closes.
Compares implementation against spec to find requirement gaps.

8 tasks: flowctl fields, command shim, codex command, skill, ralph gate,
guard handling, docs, work skill update.

Closes #83

fix(flow-next): enforce all scouts in plan skill + document --deps flag

- Add CRITICAL block requiring ALL scouts to run in parallel
- Document existing --deps flag on task create (was undocumented in skill)
- Update step 7 to show --deps as preferred over separate dep add calls
- Add anti-pattern warning for skipping scouts
chore(flow-next): release v0.19.0 - worker review enforcement

Merge branch 'fix/worker-review-enforcement'

* fix/worker-review-enforcement:
  fix(worker): enforce impl-review with hook guidance
  fix(worker): enforce impl-review completion before returning

fix(worker): enforce impl-review with hook guidance

Simplified worker.md enforcement (vs original PR):
- Phase 4 header: "MANDATORY if REVIEW_MODE != none"
- Clear instruction: must invoke impl-review and receive SHIP
- Added rule: "Review before done"

Improved ralph-guard.py stop hook:
- Now tells worker to invoke review skill, not write receipt manually
- Prevents bypassing actual review
- Worker can continue in-context after correction (no full retry)

Original issue: Worker skipped Phase 4 (review), ran flowctl done,
returned early. Ralph blocked on missing receipt, forced retries.

Co-authored-by: Tiago Freitas <tiago@scarletai.co>
docs(ralph): document --config flag

feat(ralph): add --config flag for alternate config file

Allows specifying custom config: `ralph.sh --config my-codex-config.env`
Useful for different platforms/review backends without editing config.env.

Pre-scans args before sourcing to handle --config early.
Validates: missing value, flag-as-value, file existence.
Handles paths with spaces, relative paths, Windows paths.

Closes #82

feat(ralph): add version check warning at startup

Ralph now compares .flow/meta.json setup_version vs plugin version.
Shows warning if outdated, prompting user to run /flow-next:setup.
Non-blocking - warn only, same pattern as flow-next:plan/interview.

fix(ralph): block Explore auto-delegation in autonomous mode

Worker has disallowedTools: Task but enforcement is buggy (CC #21295, #21296).
When Explore was auto-spawned, it failed with READ-ONLY constraint and couldn't
write receipts, causing infinite retry loops.

Now explicitly block Task(Explore) at CLI level in ralph.sh. CLI precedence (2)
beats agent frontmatter (6). Interactive mode unaffected.

Ref: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents#disable-specific-subagents

fix(worker): enforce impl-review completion before returning

## Problem

Worker subagent returns early without invoking `/flow-next:impl-review`
when REVIEW_MODE is `rp` or `codex`. This causes:
- Ralph to block on missing review receipt
- ralph-guard to reject receipt writes (no review done)
- Tasks stuck in 5-retry loop then blocked

### Observed behavior:
1. Worker completes implementation (writes files, commits)
2. Worker runs `flowctl done`
3. Worker returns WITHOUT invoking `/flow-next:impl-review`
4. Stop hook blocks: "Review receipt not written"
5. Ralph forces retry, same thing happens 5 times
6. Task auto-blocked

## Solution

Add multiple checkpoints in worker.md to prevent early return:

1. **Top-level warning**: "CRITICAL: DO NOT RETURN EARLY" with checklist
2. **Phase 4 header**: Changed to "MANDATORY if REVIEW_MODE is rp or codex"
3. **Pre-Phase 5 gate**: Explicit STOP check before proceeding
4. **Phase 6 final check**: Another reminder before returning
5. **Rules update**: Added "Review before done" rule

These reinforcements make it harder for the model to skip the review
phase when review is required.
docs: update expired Discord invite link
chore: update local flowctl to v0.18.24
chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.18.24

fix(ralph): close epics before selector to unblock dependent epics (#79)

## Problem

When Ralph completes all tasks of an epic that has dependent epics,
those dependent epics remain blocked until the next iteration because
`maybe_close_epics()` is called AFTER the selector returns "none".

### Race condition timeline:
1. Ralph completes last task of epic A (e.g., fn-8-lfy)
2. Next iteration: selector runs
3. Selector sees epic A status="open" (not closed yet)
4. Selector sees epic B (fn-9-usj) blocked by A
5. Selector returns status="none" (no work available)
6. `maybe_close_epics()` closes epic A
7. Ralph exits with NO_WORK
8. Epic B never gets picked up for plan review

### Real-world impact:
In a multi-epic run with dependencies (e.g., infrastructure epic
followed by feature epics), Ralph would complete the first epic
then exit, leaving all dependent epics untouched.

## Solution

Move `maybe_close_epics()` to the START of each iteration, before
calling the selector. This ensures:
1. Completed epics are closed before selector runs
2. Dependent epics are immediately unblocked
3. Selector correctly returns the next epic for plan review

## Testing

Verified with a 12-epic dependency chain where fn-9-usj through
fn-19-p5j all depend on fn-8-lfy. After the fix:
- `flowctl next --require-plan-review` correctly returns fn-9-usj
- Ralph continues to the next epic instead of exiting

fix(ralph): close epics before selector to unblock dependent epics (#79)

## Problem

When Ralph completes all tasks of an epic that has dependent epics,
those dependent epics remain blocked until the next iteration because
`maybe_close_epics()` is called AFTER the selector returns "none".

### Race condition timeline:
1. Ralph completes last task of epic A (e.g., fn-8-lfy)
2. Next iteration: selector runs
3. Selector sees epic A status="open" (not closed yet)
4. Selector sees epic B (fn-9-usj) blocked by A
5. Selector returns status="none" (no work available)
6. `maybe_close_epics()` closes epic A
7. Ralph exits with NO_WORK
8. Epic B never gets picked up for plan review

### Real-world impact:
In a multi-epic run with dependencies (e.g., infrastructure epic
followed by feature epics), Ralph would complete the first epic
then exit, leaving all dependent epics untouched.

## Solution

Move `maybe_close_epics()` to the START of each iteration, before
calling the selector. This ensures:
1. Completed epics are closed before selector runs
2. Dependent epics are immediately unblocked
3. Selector correctly returns the next epic for plan review

## Testing

Verified with a 12-epic dependency chain where fn-9-usj through
fn-19-p5j all depend on fn-8-lfy. After the fix:
- `flowctl next --require-plan-review` correctly returns fn-9-usj
- Ralph continues to the next epic instead of exiting

commit.message:
chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.18.20

Task sizing guidance: M is the sweet spot, not S.

- Plan: prefer M tasks, combine sequential S tasks, 7+ = over-split
- Examples: OAuth 4→2 tasks, added over-split anti-pattern
- Review: criterion #8 flags over-splitting
- Interview: balance split vs combine questions

feat(flow-next): enable memory and planSync by default

- memory.enabled: true (was false)
- planSync.enabled: true (was false)
- crossEpic remains false to avoid long Ralph loops
- Updated setup workflow to recommend Yes for both

docs(flow-next): clarify all rm -rf commands are manual operations

Add "run manually" notes to all uninstall/cleanup instructions:
- README: cleanup and uninstall sections
- ralph.md: uninstall section
- SKILL.md: ralph-init output
- workflow.md: setup output

DCG blocks these from AI agents - this is intentional protection.

fix(flow-next): uninstall prints commands instead of executing rm -rf

Aligns with DCG philosophy - destructive commands should have human hands
on keyboard. AI cleans up docs (safe), prints rm commands for user to run.

fix(flow-next): remove DCG allowlist - blocking uninstall is correct

DCG should block `rm -rf .flow/` and `rm -rf scripts/ralph/`:
- Protects user's epics, tasks, and run artifacts
- Uninstall is a manual operation, not AI-driven
- Allowlisting defeats the purpose of DCG protection
docs(flow-next): remove outdated RP 1.6.0 requirement warning

docs(flow-next): restructure ralph.md for clarity and scannability

- Add TL;DR at top
- Hierarchical ToC with nested sections
- Clean Quick Start (1-5 steps, no awkward decimals)
- Group related sections: Architecture, Quality Gates, Safety & Isolation
- Consistent formatting: blockquotes for tips/notes, tables where appropriate
- Streamlined troubleshooting with problem → check → causes format
- Better visual hierarchy and scanability

docs(flow-next): sync safety guidance between README and ralph.md

- Add community sandbox setups to ralph.md (was only in README)
- Add DCG link to README safety callout

docs(flow-next): add DCG safety guard section to Ralph docs

- Add "Additional Safety: DCG" section with installation, compatibility, allowlist config
- Include project-level .dcg/allowlist.toml with command_prefix rules for flow-next cleanup
- Document uninstall steps for dcg

fix(flowctl): Fix Codex sandbox mode on Windows + stale receipt handling (#74)

* fix(flowctl): Fix Codex sandbox mode on Windows + stale receipt handling

## Problem
- Codex CLI's default read-only sandbox blocks ALL shell commands on Windows
- flowctl codex impl-review/plan-review failed silently, appearing as NEEDS_WORK
- Stale receipts from previous reviews were not cleared on failures
- Ralph could pass gates with outdated SHIP verdicts

## Solution
1. Platform-aware sandbox resolution (resolve_codex_sandbox())
   - CODEX_SANDBOX env var: CLI flag > env var > platform default
   - 'auto' defaults to danger-full-access on Windows, read-only on Unix

2. Sandbox failure detection (is_sandbox_failure())
   - Detects sandbox blocking via exit code + error patterns
   - Returns exit code 3 (distinct from 0/1/2)
   - Does NOT write receipt when sandbox failure detected

3. Stale receipt cleanup on ALL failures (not just sandbox)

4. Ralph config: CODEX_SANDBOX=auto in config.env template

Bumps flow-next to 0.18.15

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: merge scripts

* fix(flowctl): address PR review feedback for Codex sandbox Windows fix

- Platform-gate file embedding: only embed on Windows (os.name == "nt"),
  keep exploration-focused prompts on Unix where sandbox works correctly
- Platform-gate review prompts: files_embedded flag controls whether prompts
  say "do NOT read files" (Windows) or "explore the codebase" (Unix)
- Document --files as breaking change in CHANGELOG (prominent section)
- Remove ralph_fix.txt and pycache from tracked files
- Update .gitignore to exclude Python bytecode

This addresses all feedback from PR review:
1. Platform-gate embedding/prompts on platform, not sandbox mode
2. Remove development artifacts from tracked files
3. Document --files breaking change prominently

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove fn-21-en1 task tracking files

These task tracking files shouldn't be part of the PR - they track
the implementation work for this fix, not the fix itself.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Per PR review feedback:
- scripts/ralph/ - local Ralph setup (templates live in plugins/flow-next/skills/flow-next-ralph-init/templates/)
- .flow/bin/__pycache__/ - compiled Python bytecode

These are generated by /flow-next:setup and /flow-next:ralph-init
and shouldn't be in the main repo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Reset .flow/epics/*.json and config to main state
- Remove fn-21-en1 task tracking (work artifacts for this fix)
- Remove .flow/memory/ (local artifacts)
- Remove .flow/bin/__pycache__/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(flow-next): preserve GH-73 COMPLETE fix + improve --files docs

- Revert ralph.sh COMPLETE handling regression: workers should
  NEVER honor COMPLETE (completion via selector status=none only)
- Improve --files guidance in plan-review skills: explain how to
  identify files (read epic spec, find affected paths) vs hardcoded example
- Bump version to 0.18.18

---------

tree 8f0c0787c8749343004f13cd26bb631529923e45
parent 8df7ddac60cf9b25234ac847691b5514a3231f19
author VexyCats <17414704+VexyCats@users.noreply.github.com> 1769337670 +0800
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1769337670 +0100

fix(flowctl): Fix Codex sandbox mode on Windows + stale receipt handling (#74)

* fix(flowctl): Fix Codex sandbox mode on Windows + stale receipt handling

## Problem
- Codex CLI's default read-only sandbox blocks ALL shell commands on Windows
- flowctl codex impl-review/plan-review failed silently, appearing as NEEDS_WORK
- Stale receipts from previous reviews were not cleared on failures
- Ralph could pass gates with outdated SHIP verdicts

## Solution
1. Platform-aware sandbox resolution (resolve_codex_sandbox())
   - CODEX_SANDBOX env var: CLI flag > env var > platform default
   - 'auto' defaults to danger-full-access on Windows, read-only on Unix

2. Sandbox failure detection (is_sandbox_failure())
   - Detects sandbox blocking via exit code + error patterns
   - Returns exit code 3 (distinct from 0/1/2)
   - Does NOT write receipt when sandbox failure detected

3. Stale receipt cleanup on ALL failures (not just sandbox)

4. Ralph config: CODEX_SANDBOX=auto in config.env template

Bumps flow-next to 0.18.15

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: merge scripts

* fix(flowctl): address PR review feedback for Codex sandbox Windows fix

- Platform-gate file embedding: only embed on Windows (os.name == "nt"),
  keep exploration-focused prompts on Unix where sandbox works correctly
- Platform-gate review prompts: files_embedded flag controls whether prompts
  say "do NOT read files" (Windows) or "explore the codebase" (Unix)
- Document --files as breaking change in CHANGELOG (prominent section)
- Remove ralph_fix.txt and pycache from tracked files
- Update .gitignore to exclude Python bytecode

This addresses all feedback from PR review:
1. Platform-gate embedding/prompts on platform, not sandbox mode
2. Remove development artifacts from tracked files
3. Document --files breaking change prominently

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove fn-21-en1 task tracking files

These task tracking files shouldn't be part of the PR - they track
the implementation work for this fix, not the fix itself.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove local Ralph setup and pycache from tracking

Per PR review feedback:
- scripts/ralph/ - local Ralph setup (templates live in plugins/flow-next/skills/flow-next-ralph-init/templates/)
- .flow/bin/__pycache__/ - compiled Python bytecode

These are generated by /flow-next:setup and /flow-next:ralph-init
and shouldn't be in the main repo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: sync .flow/ state with main and remove fn-21 artifacts

- Reset .flow/epics/*.json and config to main state
- Remove fn-21-en1 task tracking (work artifacts for this fix)
- Remove .flow/memory/ (local artifacts)
- Remove .flow/bin/__pycache__/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(flow-next): preserve GH-73 COMPLETE fix + improve --files docs

- Revert ralph.sh COMPLETE handling regression: workers should
  NEVER honor COMPLETE (completion via selector status=none only)
- Improve --files guidance in plan-review skills: explain how to
  identify files (read epic spec, find affected paths) vs hardcoded example
- Bump version to 0.18.18

---------

Co-authored-by: VexyCats <vexycats>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Gordon Mickel <gordon.mickel@gmail.com>

commit.message:
fix(flowctl): filter artifact files using is_task_id() validation (#77)

* fix(flowctl): filter artifact files using is_task_id() validation

When listing tasks for an epic, the glob pattern (e.g., fn-1-418.*.json)
matches both task files (fn-1-418.5.json) and artifact files
(fn-1-418.5-review.json). The previous filter ("." not in task_id) only
skipped epic files but not artifact files.

This caused KeyError: 'title' crashes when artifact files were processed,
as they have an 'id' field (passing the GH-21 check) but missing required
fields like 'title'.

Fix: Replace the weak "." not in task_id check with is_task_id(task_id)
which validates the filename against the task ID regex pattern. This
correctly filters out artifact files like fn-1.2-review.json because
they don't match the expected fn-N[-xxx].M format.

Affected commands: show, tasks, epics, ready, close, validate, checkpoint,
migrate-state, and status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: also update find_dependents to use is_task_id()

* chore(flow-next): bump version to 0.18.17

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Gordon Mickel <gordon.mickel@gmail.com>

* fix(flowctl): filter artifact files using is_task_id() validation

When listing tasks for an epic, the glob pattern (e.g., fn-1-418.*.json)
matches both task files (fn-1-418.5.json) and artifact files
(fn-1-418.5-review.json). The previous filter ("." not in task_id) only
skipped epic files but not artifact files.

This caused KeyError: 'title' crashes when artifact files were processed,
as they have an 'id' field (passing the GH-21 check) but missing required
fields like 'title'.

Fix: Replace the weak "." not in task_id check with is_task_id(task_id)
which validates the filename against the task ID regex pattern. This
correctly filters out artifact files like fn-1.2-review.json because
they don't match the expected fn-N[-xxx].M format.

Affected commands: show, tasks, epics, ready, close, validate, checkpoint,
migrate-state, and status.

commit.message: chore: update flow-next setup to 0.18.16

feat(flow-next): add parallelization guidance for task splitting

- Plan skill: guidance to minimize file overlap for parallel work
- Plan-review: added "Parallelizability" criterion (#3)
- Interview: probe for disjoint file scopes under Architecture

releases.name: flow-next v0.26.0
releases.body:
### Changed

- **Codex model defaults: gpt-5.4 across the board** — review/oracle model upgraded from `gpt-5.2` to `gpt-5.4` (high reasoning). Agent intelligent tier upgraded from `gpt-5.3-codex` to `gpt-5.4` (high reasoning). Fast scouts remain on `gpt-5.3-codex-spark`.

### Fixed

- **Codex docs: removed incorrect Ralph support claim** — "What works" section incorrectly listed Ralph autonomous mode. Ralph requires plugin hooks (guard hooks, receipt gating) which Codex doesn't support. Expanded caveats to clarify.

releases.name: flow-next v0.25.0
releases.body:
### Fixed

- **Codex reviews: embed files on all platforms** — removed `os.name == "nt"` gate that restricted file embedding to Windows only. On Unix/macOS, Codex wasted its entire turn budget reading files via `sed`/`rg` before producing a verdict (observed 114 shell commands, 3.68M tokens, no verdict on complex epics). Now always embeds changed files with budget-aware fallback: disk reads allowed when embed budget is exceeded. Default `FLOW_CODEX_EMBED_MAX_BYTES` raised from 100KB to 500KB. (Thanks @acebytes — [#93](https://github.com/gmickel/gmickel-claude-marketplace/pull/93))

releases.name: flow-next v0.24.0
releases.body:
### Added

- **Spec-driven workflow** — "create a spec for X" now has guidance in the CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md snippet installed by `/flow-next:setup`. Creates an epic with structured spec template (Goal & Context, Architecture & Data Models, API Contracts, Edge Cases & Constraints, Acceptance Criteria, Boundaries, Decision Context). Then choose `/flow-next:plan` (task breakdown) or `/flow-next:interview` (refine spec).
- **README: spec-driven entry point** — new "Spec-driven" workflow section in "When to Use What", updated summary table, clarified Spec vs Interview vs Plan boundaries.

> Re-run `/flow-next:setup` to update your project's CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md with the new spec guidance.

releases.name: flow-next v0.23.0
releases.body:
### Added

- **Browser skill: comprehensive update from upstream agent-browser** — synced with latest `vercel-labs/agent-browser` skill. New features: version check on use, command chaining guidance, `snapshot -i -C` (cursor-interactive), `click --new-tab`, diff commands (snapshot/screenshot/url comparison), annotated screenshots (`--annotate` vision mode), safe JS eval (`--stdin`/`-b`), config file support, session persistence with encryption (`--session-name`), `--auto-connect` for existing Chrome, `--allow-file-access` for local files, iOS Simulator (`-p ios`), timeouts section, `get box`/`get styles`, `drag`/`upload`, video recording, Chrome DevTools profiling.
- **Browser skill: new reference files** — `commands.md` (full command reference), `snapshot-refs.md` (ref lifecycle/notation), `session-management.md` (auto-persistence/encryption/concurrency), `proxy.md` (proxy config/geo-testing/rotating proxies).
- **Browser skill: updated references** — `auth.md` (OAuth/SSO, 2FA, token refresh, security best practices), `debugging.md` (video recording, profiling), `advanced.md` (auto-connect, extensions, env vars, eval stdin/base64).

releases.name: flow-next v0.22.3
releases.body:
### Fixed

- **RP `--create` fails on empty default window** — when only an empty RP window exists (no folder loaded), `setup-review --create` reused it instead of creating a workspace with the repo folder, causing "No workspace open" from the builder. Now falls through to workspace creation.

readme_html:
gmickel claude marketplace

Plugins that make AI agents actually work.

💬 Join the Discord — discussions, updates, feature requests, bug reports

🔄 Update issues? Run: claude plugin update flow-next@gmickel-claude-marketplace

🆕 /flow-next:epic-review: Epic-completion review gate. Catches requirement gaps that per-task reviews miss—before epic closes.

🤖 Ralph mode: Ship features while you sleep. Fresh context per iteration, multi-model review gates, auto-blocks stuck tasks.

📡 Cross-platform reviews: RepoPrompt (macOS) or Codex CLI (any OS) · Codex install

🧪 OpenCode user? Try flow-next-opencode (experimental port)

[!TIP] 🤖 Works on Factory Droid too! Claude Code plugins are fully compatible:

droid plugin marketplace add https://github.com/gmickel/gmickel-claude-marketplace

Then /plugins → Marketplace → install flow-next. Commands don't autocomplete yet but work when typed (e.g. /flow-next:plan). Skills load automatically.

[!TIP] 🖥️ OpenAI Codex user? Full multi-agent support (20 roles, parallel scouts, 3-tier model mapping):

git clone https://github.com/gmickel/gmickel-claude-marketplace.git
cd gmickel-claude-marketplace && ./scripts/install-codex.sh flow-next

Requires Codex 0.102.0+. Commands use /prompts: prefix (e.g. /prompts:plan). See Codex install guide.

The Problem

Process failures, not model failures:

  • Starting to code before understanding the codebase
  • Reinventing patterns already there
  • Forgetting the plan mid-implementation
  • Skipping edge cases obvious in hindsight

This marketplace ships plugins that fix these problems.

Flow-Next

Plan first, work second. Zero external dependencies.

# Install
/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/gmickel/gmickel-claude-marketplace
/plugin install flow-next

# Setup (configures review backend + CLI tools)
/flow-next:setup

# Use
/flow-next:plan Add a contact form with validation
/flow-next:work fn-1

Why It Works

Problem Solution
Context drift Re-anchoring before EVERY task — re-reads specs + git state from .flow/
200K token limits Fresh context per task — worker subagent starts clean each task
Single-model blind spots Cross-model reviews — RepoPrompt or Codex as second opinion
Forgotten requirements Dependency graphs — tasks declare blockers, nothing runs out of order
"It worked on my machine" Evidence recording — commits, test output, PRs tracked per task
Infinite retry loops Auto-block stuck tasks — fails after N attempts, moves on
Team conflicts Multi-user safe — scan-based IDs, soft claims, no coordination server

Commands

Command What It Does
/flow-next:plan Research codebase, create epic + tasks
/flow-next:work Execute tasks with re-anchoring
/flow-next:interview Deep spec refinement (40+ questions)
/flow-next:plan-review Cross-model plan review
/flow-next:impl-review Cross-model implementation review
/flow-next:prime Assess codebase agent-readiness, propose fixes
/flow-next:ralph-init Scaffold autonomous loop

📖 Full documentation — CLI reference, workflow details, troubleshooting

🤔 Confused when to use Interview vs Plan vs Work? See When to Use What

Ralph (Autonomous Mode)

Run overnight, walk away. Fresh context per iteration + multi-model review gates.

/flow-next:ralph-init           # One-time setup
scripts/ralph/ralph.sh          # Run from terminal

How Ralph differs:

Aspect Typical Agents Ralph
Context Accumulates (drift) Fresh each iteration
Review Self-review only Cross-model gates
Stuck tasks Infinite retry Auto-block after N failures
Validation Tests only Tests + receipts + reviews

📖 Ralph deep dive — guard hooks, receipt gating, sentinel controls

🖥️ Ralph TUI — Terminal UI for monitoring (bun add -g @gmickel/flow-next-tui)

Install

# Add marketplace
/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/gmickel/gmickel-claude-marketplace

# Install flow-next
/plugin install flow-next

# Setup (configures review backend + CLI tools)
/flow-next:setup

Other Plugins

Plugin Status
flow-next Active development. Recommended.
flow Legacy. Documentation

Ecosystem

Project Platform
flow-next-opencode OpenCode
FlowFactory Factory.ai Droid

Also Check Out

GNO — Local hybrid search for your notes, docs, and code. Give Claude Code long-term memory over your files via MCP.

bun install -g @gmickel/gno && gno mcp install --target claude-code

Contributing

  1. Create plugins/<name>/ with .claude-plugin/plugin.json
  2. Add commands/agents/skills under that plugin root
  3. Update .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
  4. Validate: jq . .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

Made by Gordon Mickel · @gmickel · gordon@mickel.tech
